title: Pliith
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Species |image= |name=Pliith |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]]{{JournalCite|1|Breaking Free: The Adventures of Dannen Lifehold}} |class=[[Plant/Legends|Plant]] |subspecies= |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=Purple fronds when angry |haircolor= |feathers= |eyecolor= |distinctions= |lifespan= |origin= |habitat= |diet= |language= }} {{Quote|How can you say I sent them to kill you? You're one of my best couriers.''
"''Because they were stupid enough to use rope guns, that's why. They're still in development. I tested them myself, and I know what the cord feels like. That's how I know, you sap-blooded Pliith weed. You tried to have me killed, and make it look like a robbery.|Linkaas and Dannen Lifehold|}} The '''Pliith''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Plant/Legends|plants]]. Lacking independent locomotion, the species was dependent on other species for movement. Their plant-like physiology required substantial amounts of sunshine, together with rain and [[Fertilizer/Legends|fertilizer]]. A notable Pliith was [[Linkaas]], [[President/Legends|president]] of [[Linkaas Corporation]]. Ruthless in his business endeavors, he tried to have his [[Courier/Legends|courier]] [[Dannen Lifehold]] [[Death/Legends|killed]] so that he could retain the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] he had exchanged with the [[Human/Legends|Human]] for a substantial debt. That plan was thwarted, however, and Lifehold fled his employ. ==Biology and appearance== {{Quote|He's a plant!|Purr|}} A [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Plant/Legends|plant]] [[Species/Legends|species]], the Pliith were largely immobile—although they could twist and bend their body—and remained rooted in [[Fertilizer/Legends|fertilizer]]; consequently, they had to be wheeled around by other species for locomotion. The Pliith species required plentiful sunshine to remain healthy. When such lighting was limited, as it was during extended periods of rain on [[Evas VI]], the species utilized [[sunlamp]]s as a less effective alternative. The Pliith had numerous fronds that could rapidly change color to purple when angry. They had expressive [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. ==History== [[File:Lifehold.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Linkaas tried to have Dannen Lifehold (pictured) killed.]] {{Quote|I hate the way that plant does business.|Dannen Lifehold|}} The Pliith were part of the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] by at least [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of {{JournalCite|1|Breaking Free: The Adventures of Dannen Lifehold}}, by which point the Pliith had entered [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]. when the Pliith [[Linkaas]] served as [[President/Legends|president]] of [[Linkaas Corporation]]. Based out of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] world Evas VI, he had operations on other Mid Rim [[Planet/Legends|planets]],{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} such as [[Dohu VII]]. Linkaas was involved in criminal activities, such as [[Spice/Legends|spice]] trading and [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]]. He used coercive techniques to keep his workforce indebted to him. The president took a ruthless approach to failure, on occasion [[Death/Legends|executing]] workers for their falling short of goals. One of his employees was the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Dannen Lifehold]]. Lifehold was supplied a [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the [[Lifeline (YT-1300 light freighter)|''Lifeline'']], and was to work as a [[Courier/Legends|courier]] for Linkaas until the ship debt was paid off. However, on what was to be his final run to repay his debt, Linkaas arranged for a number of his thugs to ambush the Human while on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dohu VII]]. Lifehold managed to escape, however, gaining the friendship of a [[Tinnell]] named [[Purr]]. When confronted by Lifehold over [[Subspace/Legends|subspace]] [[Subspace transceiver/Legends|comm]], Linkaas denied any culpability in the assault, but as the cargo was lost during the encounter, insisted that Lifehold was still indebted to him. When he glimpsed the Tinnell, however, he offered to forgive Lifehold his debt in exchange for possession of Purr. Tinnell were renowned for their technical genius, but rare in the galaxy, and Linkaas wanted to study the species. However, Lifehold refused the deal and foiled a final assault on his life. He then quit his employment and fled from the Pliith. ==Behind the scenes== The Pliith were created by [[Dave Marron]] for his short story ''[[Breaking Free: The Adventures of Dannen Lifehold]]'', which was published in the [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 1|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 1]] in [[January]] [[1994]]. In the story, Linkaas, the sole representative of the Pliith species in ''Star Wars'' [[canon]], lived on Evas VI; however, this is not specified as the species' [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. ==Appearances== *{{JournalCite|1|Breaking Free: The Adventures of Dannen Lifehold}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' *{{SW|url=news/welcome-to-the-star-wars-jungle|text=Welcome to the ''Star Wars'' Jungle|int=Welcome to the Star Wars Jungle|nolive=1}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Botanical sentient species]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Pliith]] }}
